sections.about: This metric measures capital expenditure in specific technologies or projects to improve environmental and social impacts as a percentage of total capex. It falls under Principle 2, Essential Indicator 1 of the BRSR format. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Tata leads FY2025 at 37%, followed by Jindal at 11.33%, JSW at 2.02%, and SAIL at 1.84%. Tata’s FY2025 BRSR states that capex includes investments in low-CO₂ transition, SOx/NOx/dust reduction, water conservation, effluent treatment, solid-waste utilisation, safety, and employee welfare initiatives. Jindal’s FY2025 capex includes acid recovery, waste heat recovery boiler, rooftop/floating solar, and zero-liquid-discharge technology, while its FY2024 spike to 23.42% indicates a heavier sustainability capex cycle in that year. SAIL’s FY2025 figure is low as a percentage but the BRSR discloses ₹108 crore toward air and water emission-control systems, wastewater/sewage treatment, workplace safety, and health/sanitation initiatives.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata ranks 1st at 37%. Jindal is the closest peer at 11.33%, which is 25.67 percentage points behind Tata. Tata’s trajectory dipped from 23% consolidated in FY2023 to 18% in FY2024, then rose sharply to 37% in FY2025. This is consistent with Tata’s large decarbonisation and environmental-control investment agenda across BF-BOF assets, where meaningful transition capex is needed for low-emission production, water systems, waste utilisation, and air-emission controls. The FY2025 leadership position is a strong point for Tata’s management narrative.
sections.targets: No capex-percentage target identified for this metric. Tata’s FY2025 BRSR refers readers to its ESG Goals section; Jindal discloses a target to reach net-zero emissions by 2050 and reduce emissions by 50% before FY35, which explains the sustainability capex logic but is not a capex-ratio target.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel data is reported on a consolidated basis across all three financial years; all peers report on a standalone basis. SAIL did not separately report sustainability capex for FY2022-23 or FY2023-24; only FY2024-25 data is available. A higher percentage can reflect either higher sustainability capex or lower total capex denominator.
